extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won a crash-marred sprint in Salerno after mid-30s km/h average pace made the breakaway nonviable despite chaotic peloton conditions.
tactical_patterns: Extreme weather forcing mid-30s km/h averages paradoxically prevented breakaway success despite multiple peloton crashes reducing chase capacity.
Sprint teams Alpecin/Trek/DSM maintained organization despite 7km crash, suggesting deep leadout rosters can absorb mid-stage attrition better than expected.
Evenepoel crashed twice (dog incident plus finale) yet retained GC position via neutral racing protocol, exposing vulnerability without time loss.

rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: neutral: targeted KOM points from early break then soft-pedaled back, suggesting stage-hunting rather than GC support role for Groupama-FDJ
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: two crashes in one stage including pavement impact suggests positioning deficiencies in technical wet conditions persist from 2022
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: recovered from 7km crash to rejoin peloton, showing crisis management superior to trapped sprinters like Ackermann/Gaviria
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: back wheel slipping on white line in finale indicates technical skill decline in wet sprinting despite reaching contested position
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: second place while defending Maglia Ciclamino shows Lidl-Trek sprint train functioning despite intermediate sprint point leakage to Cort Nielsen
